what_it_does: Records the resolution model for how design DNA values resolve through roles, axes, cascades, composition, dials, and derivation rules, including corrections, status, and open work.
observations: States that the system, not any single use case, is the organizing principle.
Defines DERIVE, NEVER PLACE as a binding test for whether values are legitimately resolved.
Separates DNA values, roles, axes, edges, dials, and physical anchors into distinct concepts.
Defines four axes so far: containment, temporal, type, and context.
Distinguishes cascade for nested containment from compose for non-nesting axes.
Retracts earlier claims about surface/projection/reader axes and records the correction in place.
Treats roles and dials as registries that should be declared and resolved rather than hand-passed.
Tracks implementation status for roles, resolver behavior, dials, polarity, viewer split ratios, controls, marks, and remaining edge/tier work.
standouts: The file is explicitly correction-preserving: wrong earlier models are retained as signal rather than erased.
Context is narrowed to legal slot inputs rather than purpose or use.
Surface and projection are placed under the type axis as cousin-types, not independent axes.
The document repeatedly separates chosen dial positions from derived resolved values.
conventions: Uses uppercase principle labels such as SYSTEM, DERIVE, CASCADE, COMPOSE, ROLE, AXES, and DIALS.
Uses numbered sections for model concepts and a status ledger for work items.
Uses provenance notes, corrections, and epistemic qualifiers such as "so far", "open", and "tentative".
Marks status with check, yellow, and empty-square indicators.
Keeps theoretical claims tied to implementation-facing registry and resolver implications.
concerns: The document mixes model specification, historical correction log, implementation status, and future work in one file, making its active operational contract harder to isolate.
Some named concepts remain tentative inside the same document that also describes live implementation state.
The status section is dense and may be difficult to update without accidentally changing theory, history, and implementation notes together.
notes: Briefing note: this file is the local resolution-model ledger that explains how design values should derive through roles, axes, dials, cascade, and composition while preserving correction history.
questions: Which parts of the tentative type/context naming are still open versus now accepted?
What exact evidence would confirm the base-unit anchor derivation?
Where is the boundary between this file's model claims and implementation status meant to sit over time?
How will rebuilt edge definitions be validated as true signed axis-traversals?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it explains the resolution model, records corrections, and tracks related implementation work and unresolved questions.
intention_signals: It seems intended to stabilize a shared mental model for resolving design DNA without hardcoded case-specific shortcuts, while keeping unresolved theory and implementation work visible.
novelty: It treats a design system as a signed multi-axis resolution model with explicit correction history, derivation law, role-addressed dials, and legal-slot context rather than as a token catalog alone.
contribution: Contributes a neutral resolution framework: values are substance, roles address function, containment cascades, other axes compose, dials modify role-targeted families, and unresolved claims remain visible with provenance.
summary_for_embedding: docs/RESOLUTION.md is a provenance-heavy design-system theory document defining how design DNA values resolve through roles, four axes, cascade, composition, dials, physical anchors, and derive-never-place constraints.
intention_for_embedding: The file was written to preserve and operationalize Tim Geldard's resolution model while recording corrections, implementation status, and open work for future encoding.
